item.title: Judge temporarily blocks DOJ work on $1.7+ billion "anti-weaponization" fund
item.description: The Justice Department announced the $1.7 billion fund as part of a settlement of a civil lawsuit President Trump brought against the IRS.

item.title: Shrey Parikh wins 2026 Scripps spelling bee after correctly spelling 32 words in 90 seconds
item.description: Shrey Parikh, a 14-year-old eighth grader from Rancho Cucamonga, California, won the 98th annual Scripps National Spelling Bee. Lilia Luciano reports.

item.title: Jill Biden says Joe Biden's prostate cancer diagnosis was "just shocking"
item.description: Former first lady Jill Biden said it was "just shocking" when her husband, former President Joe Biden, was diagnosed with prostate cancer last year. When Rita Braver asked whether a White House physician should've caught it, Biden replied, "I do feel we had amazing care in the White House, but somehow that was missed." Watch more of the interview on Sunday.

item.title: Woman without right hand ticketed for holding phone says she "felt very uncomfortable"
item.description: Katie Thomas, a Florida woman who is missing her right hand, is reacting to a stunning traffic stop video that emerged showing an encounter where an officer alleged she was texting and driving - using her right hand. Cristian Benavides has more.

item.title: U.S. and Iran "not there yet" on a deal but "very close," Vance says
item.description: With the Strait of Hormuz still gridlocked and war raging in Lebanon, Iran and the U.S. both say there's no agreement yet.

item.title: Jill Biden biography co-author reacts to her comments on Joe Biden's debate debacle
item.description: Former First Lady Jill Biden's comments on former President Joe Biden's debate performance are contradicting her initial reaction in 2024. Associated Press White House reporter Darlene Superville, a Jill Biden biography co-author, joins CBS News with more insight.

item.title: AI warfare is here and the U.S. Army is practicing for it, robots and all
item.description: In the U.S. military's latest war games, AI took a front seat. A top commander told CBS News "it's not going to go away, and we ignore it at our own peril."

item.title: Data center frenzy is spurring a jobs boomlet for blue-collar workers
item.description: The rush to build thousands of U.S. data centers is driving demand for some workers, though economists project fewer permanent jobs.

item.title: Chicago teen detained by ICE, taken to 6 states, describes arrest: "They didn't tell me why"
item.description: Ricardo Hernandez-Navarrete, 18, was detained by ICE when he and his mom attended a routine immigration appointment. He just graduated high school after months in custody in six different states. Camilo Montoya-Galvez reports.

item.title: Book excerpt: "Marilyn: The Lost Photographs, The Last Interview"
item.description: To mark the centenary of Marilyn Monroe, her last interview and last formal photo shoot, for Life Magazine writer Richard Meryman and photographer Allan Grant, are now presented in an expanded edition for the first time.

item.title: DOJ investigating outside funding E. Jean Carroll received for lawsuits against Trump
item.description: The Department of Justice is investigating the outside funding that Trump accuser E. Jean Carroll received for her civil lawsuits against the president. CBS News' Katrina Kaufman reports.

item.title: Laos miners remain trapped in cave with oxygen running low
item.description: Rescuers may have to teach at least five miners trapped in a Laos cave to scuba dive as they run out of ways to extract them. Matt Gutman reports.

item.title: 5 dead, dozens injured as bus plows into vehicles in Virginia
item.description: The five deaths came in vehicles that were struck by the bus when it did not slow down for traffic.

item.title: Bondi testifies behind closed doors in House Epstein probe
item.description: Former Attorney General Pam Bondi is testifying before the House Oversight Committee on Friday about her handling of the Epstein files.

item.title: U.S. Army practices for warfare with AI and robots
item.description: The U.S. military conducted exercises in the Moroccan desert to explore the future of warfare, and artificial intelligence took center stage. CBS News' Chris Livesay saw the Army use AI tools to help zero in on targets, and a robot leading forces into a mock battle. A senior commander told CBS News AI is "not going to go away, and we ignore it at our own peril."

item.title: Many artists drop out of Freedom 250 concerts shortly after lineup announced
item.description: Many artists announced for the Freedom 250 concert series in Washington, D.C., this summer, say they won't be performing.

item.title: Potential U.S.-Iran ceasefire extension as deal talks progress
item.description: There's progress toward a 60-day memorandum of understanding between the U.S. and Iran, but a deal has not been reached yet. Imtiaz Tyab reports.

item.title: Did U.S. and Iran reach a deal to end the war?
item.description: Members of the Trump administration are walking back reports that the U.S. and Iran reached a memorandum of understanding to begin the process to end the war. CBS News' Aaron Navarro reports.

item.title: Blue Origin rocket explodes on launch pad in Florida
item.description: Blue Origin, which is owned by Amazon founder Jeff Bezos, was gearing up for a June launch to put a batch of Amazon "Leo" internet satellites into orbit.

item.title: Jill Biden says Joe Biden "was slowing down," but she never saw him in cognitive decline
item.description: Former first lady Jill Biden told CBS Sunday Morning's Rita Braver she didn't see signs her husband, former President Joe Biden, was in cognitive decline during his reelection campaign, but said, "he was slowing down." Ed O'Keefe has more.

item.title: At least 3 dead in Dallas apartment complex explosion
item.description: An apartment complex in Dallas exploded following a report of a gas leak. At least three people were killed and some are missing, officials say. Jason Allen reports.

item.title: Blue Origin rocket explodes in spectacular fireball
item.description: An unmanned rocket owned by Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin exploded on a Cape Canaveral launchpad as testing occurred before a planned June launch. Mark Strassmann reports.

item.title: 4 climbers fall on America's tallest peak as rescuers try to reach them
item.description: The climbers' conditions weren't immediately known, and rangers were seeking a weather window to reach the area by helicopter.

item.title: Kenyan court blocks opening of U.S. Ebola quarantine center on air base
item.description: U.S. government plans to open a quarantine center for Americans exposed to Ebola on an air base in Kenya have been temporarily halted by a court order.

item.title: Air traffic control run by Compaq computers is safe but inefficient, FAA head says
item.description: As the summer travel season starts to take off, FAA Administrator Bryan Bedford tells CBS News he has confidence in the system, despite hundreds of FAA facilities being run on decades-old technology.

item.title: Ebola, hantavirus outbreaks raise questions about Trump's health agency cuts
item.description: Infectious disease specialists say the viruses are unlikely to become pandemics, but some are still raising concerns about the federal health response.

item.title: Shrey Parikh of California wins 2026 Scripps National Spelling Bee in spell-off
item.description: Shrey Parikh of Rancho Cucamonga, California, emerged victorious Thursday in the 98th annual Scripps National Spelling Bee.

item.title: U.S. setting up Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya, officials say
item.description: The U.S. is in the process of setting up a quarantine facility in Kenya for Americans who were exposed to Ebola​ or infected with the deadly illness.

item.title: Woman missing right hand speaks out about viral traffic stop video
item.description: Kathleen Thomas, who is missing her right hand, thought it was a misunderstanding when a deputy pulled her over for allegedly holding her phone with that hand while driving. Bodycam video shows the viral interaction.

item.title: High-yield savings accounts: Best rates and top picks for May 2026
item.description: Today's high-yield savings accounts are offering hefty returns on your deposits. Here's where your money belongs now.
